This salad sets fast.

title: Jane'S Crab Cakes
ingredients: 1 pinch salt and fresh ground pepper, to taste
2 pounds lump or backfin crabmeat (well-picked for shell)
instructions: In a large bowl combine egg, mayo, horseradish, mustard, salt & pepper, Old Bay, Worcestershire sauce and Tabasco.
Toss mixture gently with crab and saltines.
Form the mixture into 3/4 inch patties.
Refrigerate for an hour, if possible.
Heat oil and butter until foam subsides.
Saute cakes 6-8 minutes a side, until golden. Drain on paper towel.
Enjoy!

title: "Any Idiot" Lemon Pie
instructions: Mix together the lemonade and milk.
Then fold in Cool Whip. Pour into baked pastry shell.
Refrigerate.

title: Swiss Onion Soup
instructions: In a kettle cook the onion and the garlic with the mustard and the salt in 2 tablespoons of the butter over moderate heat, stirring, for 10-12 minutes, or until the onion is softened.
Add the chicken stock and simmer the mixture, covered, for 20 minutes.
In a separate saucepan melt the remaining 3 tablespoons of butter over moderate heat, add the flour, and cook the roux, whisking, for 3 minutes.
Remove the pan from the heat, whisk in the scalded milk, and simmer the mixture, whisking, for 7 minutes.
Add the horseradish, the Sherry, and the Gruyere and combine the mixture well.
Add the cheese mixture to the onion/chicken stock kettle, stir in the pepper, soy sauce, Tabasco, and the Worcestershire sauce, and simmer the soup, stirring for 10 minutes.

title: Feta and Pumpkin Pastries
ingredients: One 4 oz (115g) piece pumpkin or winter squash, peeled and seeded
4 tbsp butter, melted, plus extra for the baking sheet
instructions: Cut the pumpkin flesh into very small dice and place in a small saucepan.
Pour in enough water to barely cover, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 5 minutes, or until tender.
Drain and let cool.
Preheat the oven to 350F (180C).
Mix the pumpkin, feta cheese, raisins, and cinnamon, and season with the pepper.
Stack the filo sheets on top of each other and cut the stack into 4 long strips, about 3in (5cm) wide for a total of 24 strips.
Cover the strips with moistened paper towels.
Lightly dust a baking sheet with flour.
Working with 1 strip at a time, brush the strip with butter.
Place a heaping teaspoonful of the pumpkin and cheese mixture about 1in (5cm) from the bottom end.
Fold over a corner of the strip diagonally to cover the filling and form a triangular pocket of filled pastry.
Working upward, keep folding diagonally, from one side to the other, to retain the triangular shape, until all the pastry is folded, making sure any gaps in the pastry are pressed closed.
Transfer the triangle to the baking sheet and cover with a damp paper towel.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients.
Butter another large baking sheet.
Arrange the triangles on the buttered sheet.
Brush with the remaining melted butter.
Bake for about 20 minutes, or until crisp and golden.
Serve hot or warm.
Variation:
Cheese and Potato Pastries: Substitute finely diced potato or sweet potato for the pumpkin.

title: Barbecue
instructions: Combine ingredients.
Cook in pressure cooker for 1 hour.

title: Spiced Sweet Potatoes
ingredients: 2 c. sliced sweet potatoes (cooked or canned)
brown sugar substitute (to equal 3 Tbsp. brown sugar)
instructions: Arrange sweet potatoes in nonstick casserole. Combine sugar substitute, salt, cinnamon and nutmeg.
Sprinkle over sweet potatoes.
Dot with margarine. Bake at 350° for 10 to 15 minutes. Exchanges:
1 bread, 1/2 fat.
Serving:
1/3 cup.

title: Cake
instructions: Mix dry ingredients, add water, oil and eggs.
Mix well.
Pour into tube pan, bake 45 minutes at 350°.

title: Turkey Guacamole Wraps
ingredients: 1 small ripe avocado, pitted and peeled
instructions: Place the first 5 ingredients in a food processor; process until smooth. Spread over tortillas. Top with turkey, onion and lettuce; roll up.

title: Sweetheart
ingredients: 1 tablespoon Beirao liquor licor beirao or other beverage of your choice
instructions: Divide the pastry into 2 squares
Placed in a tray with aluminum paper and flour it to grab.
In a pan put up the apples, sugar, liqueur, cinnamon and little pieces of almond to keep it moving.
If you feel that you are holding the bottom put a little bit of water.
Once the apples have cooked, let them cool and put the stuffing inside the 2 squares of puff pastry and close it in the shape of a small pillow.
Finally, grease the little pillows with an egg, place the almonds on top and put them in the oven. Approx. 20 minutes.

title: Chipotles In Adobo
instructions: Devein anchos and mulatos then soak in boiling water until soft.
Blend chiles in blender along with rest of oregano, garlic and cumin. Fry adobo in olive oil
Add salt and pepper to taste and then add all of the chipotles in vinegar Let cook for about 5 to 10 minutes.

title: Thanksgiving Stuffing, Stuffed In Squash
ingredients: 1 cup Onion, Chopped Into Small Pieces
1 cup Celery, Chopped Into Small Pieces
12 ounces, weight Mrs. Cubbisons Stuffing--I Used Corn Bread
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Cut squash in half and scoop out seeds and membrane. I tried a few different utensils and found a large spoon and a melon baller worked best.
Fry bacon in a large pan. Set aside to drain on a paper towel.

Remove all but 2 tablespoons of bacon grease and add onion and celery to the medium hot pan. Season with poultry seasoning, salt and pepper. Crumble bacon and add to pan, then stir in butter and cook until onions are translucent and celery is tender.
Turn off stove and add stuffing. Add chicken broth and stir. Taste for seasoning and adjust.

Arrange squash cut side up in a Pyrex roasting pan. Add 1/2 cup of water, cover with foil and bake 20 minutes. Remove foil and bake an additional 20 minutes or until tender, depending on the size of your squash.
**At this point, you could refrigerate both the squash and the stuffing and assemble the next day. Try to bring them to room temp if you do this so it won't take too long to reheat.**
Now for the assembly. Gently spoon the stuffing into the squash cavity. If the stuffing seems dry, add a little water. It will cook out.
Return to hot oven and bake for 15 minutes or until stuffing is a little crispy on top.
This will easily serve 8 people if you cut them in half. But first let everyone see how nice they look!

title: Grilled Peaches On The Half Shell
instructions: Wash and halve ripe peaches; remove pits and place each peach half, flat side up, in center of a square piece of doubled aluminum foil.
Fill cavity of each peach half with fresh or frozen raspberries or blueberries.
Sprinkle with 2 teaspoons brown sugar and 1 teaspoon lemon juice.
Wrap in foil.
Grill approximately 15 to 20 minutes over hot flame, turning once.
One peach equals one serving with 80 calories and 0 grams fat per serving.

title: Cajun Stew With Chicken And Ham Recipe
ingredients: 1 can stewed tomatoes Cajun recipe ( 14 1/2 ounces)
2 x boneless skinless chicken breast halves in bite sized pcs
1/2 x green bell pepper minced Louisiana warm pepper sauce optional
instructions: In a large saucepan, combine tomatoes, water, rice, onion, and celery; cook over medium-high heat to boiling.
Reduce heat; cover and simmer for 8 min or possibly till rice in nearly tender ( there will still be some liquid in pan).
Stir in chicken, ham and green pepper.
Cover and continue cooking till chicken is done, about 5 min.
Season with warm pepper sauce to taste.
Serve immediately.
Makes 4 servings.
NOTES : This edition of a Louisiana classic is mild sufficient for everyone in thefamily.
turn up the heat with warm pepper sauce.

title: Hamburger Buns
instructions: Dissolve dry yeast in warm water and set aside.
Bring water and sugar to boil for 5 minutes.
When cooled to lukewarm, add oil, salt and eggs.
Stir in yeast mixture.
Measure 12 cups flour after sifting and add to liquid mixture.
Set aside by 2 p.m. Knead down at 6 p.m.
At 10 p.m. shape into buns and put on tins. Leave, uncovered, and bake in morning around 5 to 6 a.m. in a 350° oven for 18 to 20 minutes.

title: Soap Bubbles
instructions: Combine all ingredients.
Shape pipe cleaners into a circle with a handle.
Blow bubbles through the pipe cleaner loop.
This is great fun!

title: Coffee-Bourbon Syrup
instructions: Bring sugar, water, and coffee to a boil in a medium saucepan over medium-high heat. Cook 5 minutes. Remove from heat; stir in bourbon.

title: Colorful Corn ‘N’ Bean Salad
ingredients: 1 can (15 ounces) black beans, rinsed and drained
instructions: In a large bowl, combine all ingredients. Cover and refrigerate until serving.

title: Babka (Without Yeast)
instructions: Combine sugar and margarine and mix well.
Add egg yolks, vanilla, lemon juice, flour and baking powder, beating continuously until well blended.
Bake in greased and floured 9-inch tube pan for 3/4 hour at 375°.

title: Cheese Soup
instructions: Cook celery, onion, water and chicken bouillon cubes about 20 minutes.
Add potatoes and mixed vegetables.
Cook about 20 minutes.
Add cream of chicken soup and Velveeta cheese.
Lower heat and cook until ready to serve.

title: Greek-Spiced Baked Shrimp
ingredients: 1 (28-ounce) can whole tomatoes in juice, drained, reserving juice, and chopped
1 1/4 pound large peeled and deveined shrimp
Accompaniments: crusty bread or steamed white rice; a green salad
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F with rack in middle.

Cook onion and garlic in oil with 1/4 teaspoon salt in a 4-quart heavy saucepan over medium heat until softened, about 5 minutes. Stir in spices and cook, stirring, 30 seconds. Add chopped tomatoes with juice and sugar and simmer, uncovered, stirring occasionally, until slightly thickened, about 20 minutes. Remove from heat.

Season shrimp with 1/8 teaspoon salt, then stir into tomato sauce. Transfer to a 2-quart shallow baking dish and top with feta. Bake until just cooked through, 18 to 20 minutes. Serve sprinkled with dill.

title: Original Praline Bacon Recipe
ingredients: 1 lb thick cut bacon (of good quality)
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F.

Place a wire rack on a sheet tray. Combine the Pecans and brown sugar. Lay the bacon side by side on the rack, place in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes, or until the bacon is sizzling and starting to brown around the edges. The object is for the bacon to cook about 3/4 of the way through before adding the topping.
Mix the 3 remaining ingredients.

Push the partially cooked bacon as close together as possible on the rack and brush with the cane syrup, this will give the topping something to grab on to. Cover generously with the Pecan/brown sugar topping. Place back in the oven for about 10 minutes more or until the topping is bubbly and the bacon is good and brown. Let cool.
As the Praline Bacon cools it will set up and have a nice chewy bite to it.

title: Sumac Chicken With Cauliflower And Brussels Sprouts
ingredients: 3 tablespoons olive or avocado oil, divided
1 small lemon, halved lengthwise and thinly sliced
1 small red onion, cut into 3/4 inch wedges
1 cup cup finely chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley or cilantro; preferably, a mix
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F.

Combine 2 Tbsp oil with sumac, 3/4 tsp salt, brown sugar, paprika, and red pepper in a medium bowl. Place cauliflower and Brussels sprouts on a foil-lined baking sheet. Add half of oil mixture; toss to coat.

Add chicken pieces and lemon slices to pan. Rub remaining oil mixture over chicken. Bake for 20 minutes. Stir vegetables. Sprinkle onion wedges over pan. Bake for 20 more minutes, or until chicken is done.
Combine remaining 1 Tbsp oil, parsley, and remaining ingredients in a small bowl. Spoon parsley mixture evenly over chicken and vegetables. Serve with warm whole-wheat couscous, if desired.

title: Vegan Oatmeal Pancakes With Banana
ingredients: 2/3 cup gluten free oats ground in blender to become oat flour
1 banana, use 1/4 in recipe, smashed and 1/4 to top pancakes
instructions: Stir batter together, may have lumps, that is OK.
let sit 1 min while you heat skillet to medium.
dissolve 1 tsp of coconut oil in skillet, pour in batter. Cook until bubbles form/pop and edges of pancake get browned, flip cook 1-2 mins more.
Top with very thin slices of banana on each pancake and serve with warm maple syrup. AMAZING! Makes approx 6 pancakes.

title: Sweetened Condensed Milk
instructions: Combine in blender or processor until smooth and slightly thickened.
Makes 14 oz.
(Same amount as can of Eagle Brand milk.)

title: Pilgrim Pie
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°.
In a large bowl, mix turkey, peas, corn, gravy, 2 tablespoons of the chives and the thyme.
Turn into shallow 2 1/2 to 3-quart baking dish; set aside.
Warm potatoes and mix in remaining chives.
Cover turkey mixture with potatoes. Bake at 400° for about 25 minutes, until turkey mixture is bubbly and potatoes begin to brown.
Makes about 6 servings.

title: Cauliflower Salad
instructions: Using a vegetable steam, cook the cauliflower whole, until fork tender, about 15 minutes.
Allow the vegetable to cool and then break into flowerets.
Toss with the dressing, salt and pepper and top with the cheese.

title: Broccoli and Snowpea Salad
instructions: Cook broccoli in boiling water for 2 minutes, or until just tender.
Refresh in cold water, drain.
Cook snow peas in boiling water for 1 minutes, then refresh in cold water.
Drain.
Combine broccoli, snow peas, bean shoots, capsicum and dressing in a large bowl.
Serve salad topped with spring onions and sesame seeds.

title: Crab Dip
instructions: Mix ingredients.
Pour into 9 x 13-inch pan and bake uncovered at 350° for 30 minutes.

title: Double Chocolate Crumble Bars
ingredients: 1 (6 oz.) pkg. semi-sweet chocolate pieces (1 c.)
instructions: In a large mixer bowl, cream sugar and butter or margarine together thoroughly.
Beat in eggs and vanilla.
Stir together flour, chopped nuts, cocoa, baking powder and salt.
Stir into egg mixture.
Spread in bottom of a greased 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan.
Bake in a 350° oven for 15 to 20 minutes or until cookies test done.
Sprinkle the marshmallows evenly atop; bake 3 minutes more.
Cool.

title: White chocolate biscotti-tini Recipe Lillytree
ingredients: Tahitian vanilla extract (1/2 4 teaspoon)/ 1/4 teaspoon almond extract
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker glass with ice and shake
Rim the chocolate glass (Barcandy) with crushed almonds.
Pour in cocktail
Garnish with Biscotti cookie

title: Blueberry Salad
ingredients: 1 can crushed pineapple, drained and saved
2 pkg. Dream Whip or carton Cool Whip
pineapple and blueberry juice plus enough water to make 4 c. liquid
instructions: Dissolve jello in hot water.
Add juice/water mixture.
Set aside 2 cups of jello mixture.
Chill until firm.
Add Cool Whip with reserved mixture when it reaches egg white consistency.
Add chopped nuts.
Spread over firm jello layer.
Chill until firm. Wait until biggest mixture is set then pour 2 cups mixture over and refrigerate.
Use a 9 x 13 x 2-inch pan.

title: Pure Banana Soft Serve
ingredients: 3 Tablespoons Chocolate Sauce Or Topping Of Choice (optional)
instructions: Freeze the sliced banana in a Tupperware container (give it a few hours, or overnight works-even a week is fine).

Place the frozen slices in a food processor and mix until it reaches a soft consistency. Spoon into serving bowls and drizzle with chocolate (or topping of your choice) and enjoy! You can also freeze it to use at a later time; the consistency will not be compromised.
Enjoy!

title: Company Shrimp Casserole
ingredients: salt and pepper to taste (most use red pepper)
instructions: Saute celery, pepper, onions and shrimp (shrimp may be cut up in smaller pieces).
Cook rice.
Mix all ingredients together, except cheese.
Bake, covered, in casserole, at 350° for about 20 minutes.
Remove from oven and cover top with grated cheese.

title: Halibut With Garlic Sauce
instructions: Season the halibut with salt, and dredge in the cornmeal to coat.
Heat the oil in a skillet over medium heat. Place the coated halibut in the skillet, and cook 5 minutes, until lightly browned on all sides.

Mix the chicken broth, garlic, and bay leaf in a pot, and bring to a boil. Pour into the skillet with the halibut. Season with pepper. Continue cooking 5 minutes, or until fish is easily flaked with a fork.

title: Honey(Without Bees)
instructions: Boil sugar, water and alum about 12 minutes (rolling boil). Turn off heat.
Press clover blooms in hot syrup.
Let stand 20 to 30 minutes.
Strain in cheesecloth after removing clover blooms. Pour in jars.

title: Sour Cream Enchiladas
ingredients: 17 ounces, fluid Can Green Chile Sauce
8 ounces, weight Light Or Regular Sour Cream
instructions: Combine 2 cups cheese, chicken, onions and green chilies in a bowl and set aside.
Pour enough green sauce to coat the bottom of the casserole dish.
Lay tortilla flat onto workspace and fill with chicken mixture.
Top mixture with a dollop of sour cream.
Roll once, tuck in sides and roll to close.
Place seam side down into sauced dish.
Repeat until all tortillas are filled.
Pour remaining green sauce over filled tortillas and top with remaining cheese.
Bake at 375 degrees for 15 to 20 minutes or until cheese is bubbly.

title: Quick Chicken Quesadillas
instructions: Prepare marinade by combining pineapple juice, soy sauce and garlic in a medium sized bowl. Season chicken with salt and pepper, add to marinade. Cover and refrigerate for at least 10 minutes.

Heat oil in a large skillet over medium high heat. Add sliced onions and cook until translucent. Remove onions from pan; set aside. Add chicken breast and cook on each side for 5 minutes or until cooked through. Return onions to pan and heat through. They will become caramelized when done.
Dice cooked chicken into 1/2 inch cubes. Equally divide and arrange chicken, onions and cheese on half of each tortilla.
Wipe skillet clean. Fold tortilla in half and toast in skillet over medium high heat. Flip to toast other side.
Cut each tortilla into 4 quesadillas. Serve with salsa or pico de gallo.

title: Corn Pancakes
instructions: Mix together flour, baking powder, salt, pepper, milk and egg. Add corn and mix well.
Brush large skillet or griddle with butter and drop mixture by tablespoons, a few at a time.
Cook until golden brown, turn and cook on other side.
Keep warm in oven, loosely covered, until all are done.
Serves 4 to 6.

title: Asian-Style Pork Tenderloin With Slaw
ingredients: 2 tablespoons chopped salted peanuts or cashews
Additional minced fresh cilantro and chopped peanuts
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk the first ten ingredients until blended. Pour 1 cup mixture into an ungreased 13x9-in. baking dish; add pork, turning to coat. Refrigerate, covered, 1 hour. Cover and refrigerate remaining soy sauce mixture for slaw.
Preheat oven to 450&deg. Bake pork, uncovered, 20-25 minutes or until a thermometer reads 145°, turning once. Remove from oven; tent with foil. Let stand 5 minutes before slicing.

Meanwhile, place coleslaw mix, 1/2 cup cilantro, mint and 2 tablespoons peanuts in a large bowl. Drizzle with reserved soy sauce mixture and toss to combine; serve with pork. Sprinkle with additional cilantro and peanuts.

title: Vegetable Soup
instructions: Brown beef.
Add remaining ingredients.
Simmer until vegetables are done.
Best made the day before.

title: Chocolate Peanut Clusters
instructions: Mix the first 4 ingredients.
Pour into saucepan; bring to a rolling boil, stirring often.
Lower heat and keep stirring while mixture boils slowly for 3 minutes.
Remove from heat.
Stir in peanuts.
Stir mixture until candy starts to thicken.
Drop mixture quickly onto wax paper and cool.

title: Corn Bread
ingredients: 1 eneg egg substitute (for non vegans) or 1 one egg (for non vegans)
instructions: Heat oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Mix the dry ingredients and then add the wet ones.
Whisk with a whisk.
Oil a pan that is around 8x8 - 9x9 and pour in the batter.
Bake for 30 minutes until nice and golden color and solid on the top.
No wet spots.
Optional - You may add in some japaleno peppers or canned corn into the recipe.
It can add some texture to the bread.

title: Sabzi Spinach
ingredients: 1 can of vegetable broth, bouillon powder or cubes
instructions: In a saucepan, put the oil to medium heat.
Meanwhile, wash the green onions and chop them, also chop the garlic and the cilantro.
Add everything to the oil once hot.
Once everything is cooked, transparent like, add the frozen spinach and continue to cook.
Turn around spinach every 5 minutes to continue to cook until it is no longer frozen.
Once spinach is ready and mixed with the other vegetables, add the spices.
Mix well and put the broth to stop the frying.
Cook until water is almost all evaporated and turn off the stove top.
Let stand for five minutes before serving.
You can add shredded parmessan cheese on top if you wish.
Very good recipe, healthy, light and nutricious!
A good friend of mine made it for me once and I always make it since then, my kids love it with cheese on top!

title: Southern Pork Barbecue
instructions: In large crock-pot, boil pork for 8 to 10 hours.
Use enough water to cover meat. Cool pork overnight. Reserve broth.

title: Prune Cake
instructions: Grease and flour tube cake pan.
Mix sugar and Wesson oil. Beat eggs in cup.
Add and mix sugar and oil.

title: Flo's Mac and Cheese
ingredients: 23 lb macaroni, cooked till 2/3 done (I love to use elbow macaroni!)
1 14 lbs cooper sharp cheese, shredded (or cut into small cubes)
instructions: Melt butter over low heat and add flour and milk.
Cook over low/medium heat until the mixture thickens.
Add the cheese and stir until melted (the mixture will be thick).
Add the macaroni and bake at 375 until brown.
You can also combine the macaroni and cheese sauce and put into a crockpot on low for 3 hours or until slightly browned around the edges.

title: Boston Brown Bread
instructions: Boil water and raisins for 5 minutes and cool.
Sift together the flour, salt and soda.
Cream together the shortening, sugar and egg.
Add raisin mixture to dry ingredients.
Fill 3 medium cans.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

title: Chicken-Seafood Paella
instructions: Saute mushrooms, celery, onion, and garlic in oil in a heavy ovenproof Dutch oven until tender.
Add rice; cook until rice is straw colored.
Add chicken broth and next 5 ingredients.
Remove from heat; cover and bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes.
Peel and devein shrimp.
Add shrimp, chicken and Parmesan cheese to rice mixture, stirring well.
Cover and bake an additional 30 minutes or until liquid is absorbed and rice is tender.
Transfer to a large serving bowl and garnish with celery leaves, if desired.

title: Bean And Sausage Casserole
instructions: Combine beans in 2 1/2-quart dish, after draining them.
Brown sausage in a skillet and drain.
While sausage browns add beef broth, tomato sauce, cumin, salt, pepper and picante sauce to beans.
Saute garlic and onions and add them and the sausage to the beans.
Cover and bake at 325° for approximately 1 hour.
Serves 6 people. 419 calories, 3 grams fat.

title: Lamb Chops on a Bed Of Peppers and Onions
ingredients: 1 tablespoon fresh rosemary or 1 teaspoon dried
4 tablespoons plus 2 teaspoons red wine vinegar
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
4 double thick rib or loin lamb chops, trimmed of all external fat
instructions: Thinly slice the peppers and onions.
Separate onions into rings.
Mince the garlic.
Heat the oil in large skillet and saute the peppers, onions, garlic and rosemary until the peppers and onions are very soft, about 15 minutes.
Season with black pepper.
Add 4 tablespoons vinegar; cover and cook over low heat about 15 minutes longer.
Meanwhile, preheat broiler and place chops in broiler pan and place broiler pan about 4 inches from source of heat, depending on thickness of chops.
Broil chops about 12 minutes for thin chops, about 15 minutes for double-rib chops, turning once.
Sprinkle each chop with 1/2 teaspoon vinegar.

title: Homemade Sage Butter
instructions: Place the cream in your food processor bowl and turn it on to medium speed for 10-12 minutes.
Turn off and drain the butter, yes butter! that has formed during the process. Don't throw away the liquid: that is yummy buttercream you can use in thousand recipes!
Apart blend sage leaves and salt.
Add them to the butter and mix with a wood spoon.
Your butter is ready, my dear! Keep in the fridge for a week or freeze for up to 6 months!
Enjoy Delicious Melting Butter!

title: Meyer Lemon Cream
ingredients: 5 ounces butter, cut into 1 inch pieces, room temperature
instructions: 1. Place a double boiler over, not in, simmering water.
2. Combine the sugar and lemon zest and add to the top pan of double boiler.
3.Whisk in the eggs and lemon juice.
4. Whisking constantly, cook the mixture over the simmering water, until it reaches 180 degrees and cream is thickened.

5. Once the cream is thickened - you should be able to make tracks in the mixture with your whisk - take the cream off the heat and strain it into another bowl. Let the cream cool to about 140 degrees.
6. Wisk in the butter a few pieces at a time. Once all of the butter has been melted, let the mixture set for a few minutes to ensure the mixture is perfectly smooth.
Once finished, the cream may be pour it into a container. Cover top surface with plastic wrap to keep a skin from forming. Chill in the refrigerator for about half an hour before assembly.

title: Broccoli Casserole
instructions: Cook frozen broccoli as package indicates, omitting salt. Drain well.
Place broccoli in oblong dish.
Heat soup and shredded cheese.
Gradually add milk, mayonnaise and beaten egg to soup mixture, stirring until well blended.
Pour over broccoli. Combine bread crumbs and melted butter; sprinkle over mixture. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes.

title: Pecan Pie
instructions: Beat eggs thoroughly with sugar, corn syrup, salt, vanilla and melted butter.
Place pecans in unbaked pie shell.
Pour filling over them (can put pecans on top if desired).
Place pie on solid metal cookie sheet.
Bake at 375° for 25 to 30 minutes, until filling is set.

title: Cheese Strata
ingredients: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen chopped broccoli, cooked and drained
instructions: Cut 12 doughnuts and holes from bread with doughnut cutter; set aside.
Arrange remaining bread over bottom of 9 x 13-inch baking dish.
Layer cheese, broccoli and ham over top.
Arrange bread cut-outs over top.
Combine next 5 ingredients in bowl; pour over bread.
Refrigerate, covered, overnight.
Bake at 350° for 45 to 55 minutes.
Serve.

title: pulled pork
instructions: in slow cooker, combine all ingredients and mix well.
coat the meat.
cover and cook for 10 hours.
cook til pork falls apart when stirred.
put in bowl and shred with two forks.
place back in sauce.
serve with baked beans..

title: Cabbage Rolls
instructions: Cut cabbage in half; remove the heart.
Place in large pan of water.
Add 1 tablespoon salt; boil for 15 minutes.
Separate cabbage leaves (they should be bendable but not soft); let cool. Combine in a large bowl the onion salt, garlic powder, ground beef, sausage, eggs, tomato paste and Minute rice (uncooked).
Form a 4-inch (approximate) meatball inside a cabbage leaf.
Roll (tucking in at the sides).
Place in a large saucepan.
When all the cabbage rolls have been placed in saucepan, drain sauerkraut and put it on top of them.
Sprinkle on sugar; pour on tomato juice.
Cook on medium heat for 1 hour.

title: Pumpkin Spice Bars
ingredients: 2 cups solid pack pumpkin (16 oz can)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350*.
In mixer, beat eggs until frothy.
Add sugar and continue beating 2 minutes.
Blend in oil and the canned pumpkin.
Sift dry ingredients together (over the raisins) and fold dry mixture into the egg mixture.
Do not over-mix.
Pour into a greased and floured 13 x 9" pan.
Bake for 35 to 40 minutes or until done throughout.
Cool on rack, then cut into 24 squares.
For variety, top each square with a toasted walnut half.

title: Fruity Blueberry Strawberry Pulav
ingredients: 1 cup basmati rice for making strawberry flavor
1 cup basmati rice for making blueberry flavor
instructions: Wash 1 cup basmati rice and soak it in water for half an hour. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a pan, and add cumin seeds. When the seeds start to crackle, add black pepper powder. Now add blueberry puree, along with half cup of water, salt and rice. Cover the pan with a lid, and cook it on medium heat until rice is cooked well. Add more water if needed. When rice is cooked, add 1/2 tsp lemon juice, and stir well. Remove Blueberry Pulav from heat, and keep aside.

Wash 1 cup basmati rice and soak it in water for half an hour. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a pan, and add cumin seeds. When the seeds start to crackle, add black pepper powder. Now add strawberry puree, along with half cup of water, salt and rice. Cover the pan with a lid, and cook it on medium heat, until rice is cooked well. Add more water if needed. When rice is cooked, add 1/2 tsp lemon juice, and stir well. Remove Strawberry Pulav from heat, and keep aside.

Take a bowl of your choice. Add 3 tbsp blueberry pulav to form layer 1 and press it well. Now add 2 tbsp strawberry pulav to form layer 2, and press well again. For layer 3, again add 3 tbsp blueberry pulav, and press well. Transfer your rice bowl carefully to the serving palate, to keep its shape. Fruity Blueberry Strawberry Pulav is ready. Serve hot with any favorite Raita or Curry. Enjoy!

title: Pecan Pumpkin Filled Ebelskiver
ingredients: 4 oz. cream cheese, at room temperature
4 Tbs. (1/2 stick) melted unsalted butter, plus more for cooking
instructions: 1. In a bowl, using a handheld mixer fitted with the beater attachment, beat the cream cheese on medium speed until softened, about 30 seconds. Add the pecan pumpkin butter and beat until smooth and well combined, about 45 seconds. Transfer the filling to a small bowl and refrigerate until ready to use.

2. In a bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda and salt. In a small bowl, whisk the egg yolks, then whisk in the buttermilk and the 4 Tbs. butter. Whisk the yolk mixture into the flour mixture until well combined.

3. In another bowl, using an electric mixer fitted with the whisk attachment, beat the egg whites on high speed until stiff but not dry peaks form, 1 to 2 minutes. Using a rubber spatula, gently fold the whites into the batter in 2 additions.

4. Put 1/2 tsp. butter in each well of a filled-pancake pan. Set over medium-low heat and heat until the butter begins to bubble. Pour a heaping 1 Tbs. batter into each well (fill to just below the edge of the well). Cook until the undersides begin to set and turn light golden brown, about 1 minute. Using 2 wooden skewers, tilt each pancake at an angle on one edge, allowing some of the batter to flow out. Put 1 tsp. pecan pumpkin filling inside, on top of the uncooked batter, and cook for 30 seconds to 1 minute.

5. Using the skewers, tilt the pancakes again, allowing the remaining uncooked batter to flow out and enclose the filling in the center. Continue cooking the pancakes until they are evenly golden brown on all sides, turning them over if needed, 2 to 3 minutes more. Repeat with the remaining batter and filling.

title: Fettuccine with Ham and Napa Cabbage
instructions: In a large saucepan of salted boiling water cook the fettuccine until it is al dente, reserve 1/2 cup of the cooking water, and drain the pasta well.

While the pasta is cooking, in a heavy skillet cook the onion and the cabbage in the oil over moderate heat, stirring, until the vegetables are golden, stir in the ham and the caraway seeds, and cook the mixture, stirring occasionally, for 2 minutes, or until the cabbage is tender.
Add the cream and simmer the mixture for 1 minute.
In a large bowl toss together the fettuccine, the ham mixture, and salt and pepper to taste and add enough of the reserved cooking water to thin the sauce to the desired consistency.

title: Corn Flake Squares
instructions: Melt margarine and marshmallows over low heat.
Stir in cereals and coconut.
Press into a greased 8-inch square baking dish, using a greased spatula.
Let cool (in fridge, if necessary) and cut into 12 squares.

title: Ultimate Chocolate Chip Cookies
ingredients: 1-1/4 c. firmly packed light brown sugar
instructions: Heat oven to 375°. Combine shortening, brown sugar, milk and vanilla in large bowl. Beat at medium speed until well blended. Beat egg into mixture. Combine flour, salt and baking soda. Add to mixture blending well. Stir in chocolate chips and pecans. Drop rounded teaspoonfuls of dough 3 inches apart onto ungreased baking sheet. Bake 8 to 10 minutes for chewy cookies, 11 to 13 minutes for crisp cookies. Let cool.

title: Simmering Irish Stew With Dumplings
ingredients: 3 pounds lamb stew meat, cut into 1 1/2-inch cubes
1 teaspoon salt, plus more to taste
1/4 teaspoon pepper, plus more to taste
6 carrots, peeled and cut into 2-inch pieces
2 turnips, peeled and cut into 1-inch chunks
instructions: Heat oil in a large pot over high heat and brown the meat in two batches.
Return meat to the pot and add 12 cups water, 1 teaspoon salt and 1/4 teaspoon pepper.
Simmer for 1 hour.
Add carrots, turnips and onions and simmer for another hour.
Place potatoes in a small pan and ladle enough broth over them to just cover.
Simmer potatoes until just tender.
Pour stock back into meat, leaving just enough to keep potatoes moist.
Season with salt and set aside.
When vegetables and meat are very tender, strain the stew.
Let the broth set until fat accumulates on top.
Skim fat off and measure out 4 tablespoons (if you have less, use some butter).
Heat fat in the pot over medium heat.
Add flour and stir for 2 minutes.
Whisk in the broth.
Cook, stirring, until mixture thickens.
Add meat and vegetables and reduce heat to a simmer.
Whisk together flour, baking powder and salt.
Whisk in parsley.
Heat milk and butter until butter melts, stir it into the flour mixture until just combined.
Drop rounded spoonfuls onto the simmering stew.
Cover and cook for 10 minutes.
Serve with reheated potatoes.

title: Cactus Paddle Salad (Nopales Salad)
ingredients: 4 medium nopales, trimmed, scraped, cut into strips
1 tablespoon balsamic vinegar or 1 tablespoon cider vinegar
instructions: Heat 6 inches of water and 1/2 teaspoon of the salt to a boil in a Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the cactus and boil, uncovered, until tender, about 20 minutes.
Drain the cactus and transfer it to a large bowl.
Add tomato, onion and cilantro; set aside.
Whisk together the oil, vinegar, oregano and salt; pour over the vegetables and toss.
Line a platter with lettuce leaves; top with salad; sprinkle with cheese; garnish with jalapenos and radish slices.

title: Beef & Chickpea Paprikash
ingredients: 2 lbs stewing beef, cut into chunks
1 1/2 cups vegetable stock or 1 1/2 cups water
1 (14 1/2 ounce) can chickpeas, rinsed and drained
1/4 cup Greek yogurt or 1/4 cup sour cream
instructions: Toss the beef in the flour, sea salt and pepper until well-coated.

Heat the butter over medium-high heat in a heavy pan or dutch oven. Add the onion and garlic, then add the beef while shaking off excess flour coating. (Set aside any leftover flour because you might need it later) Fry, stirring occasionally, until the beef has turned brown, about 5-8 minutes.

Add the stock/water, paprika, brown sugar, cayenne pepper, mustard, ketchup and Worcestershire sauce the pot. Stir and bring to a boil, then lower heat, cover, and simmer for 2 hours. Stir occasionally.
After two hours, add the drained chickpeas and cook for a further 30 minutes.

Remove from heat. Add the yogurt/sour cream, but be sure to temper it with a bit of the warm sauce first to make sure it does not curdle. (If your stew looks watery, you can stir the leftover flour you set aside earlier into the yogurt before adding it to the pot to thicken the sauce nicely. If not, just discard the flour and add the yogurt by itself.) Stir until the paprikash turns a dark orange color.
Serve over egg noodles, pasta, rice, mashed potatoes or, my favorite- fresh gnocchi.

title: Sugar-Free Peanut Butter Cookies
instructions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Lightly grease a baking sheet.
Thoroughly mix together the peanut butter, sucralose, and eggs in a bowl. Add rest of ingredients. Drop mixture by spoonfuls onto the prepared baking sheet.
Bake in the preheated oven until center appears dry, about 8 minutes. Cool on cookie rack.

title: Sesame Pasta Salad
ingredients: 1/3 cup sesame oil (should smell slightly of sesame, light amber color)
1 teaspoon toasted sesame oil (should smell strongly of sesame, very dark color)
3 tablespoons coconut sugar crystals (or your sweetener of choice)
instructions: Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Add sesame seeds, and cook stirring frequently until lightly toasted. Remove from heat, and set aside.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add pasta, and cook for 8 to 10 minutes, or until al dente. Drain pasta, and rinse under cold water until cool. Transfer to a large bowl.
In a jar with a tight-fitting lid, combine oil, soy sauce, vinegar, sesame oil, sweetener, sesame seeds, ginger, and pepper. Shake well.
Pour sesame dressing over pasta, and toss to coat evenly. Gently mix in cabbage, cilantro, and green onions.

title: Chocolate Biscuits
instructions: Place sugar, salt, flour and cocoa in saucepan, slowly adding milk.
Cook until thick.
Add vanilla and pour over baked biscuits.

title: Brownies
instructions: Put in chocolate, flour, sugar and butter.
Cut it up.
Put it in this little block.
Put it in the oven on medium.
Cook 30 minutes.
Let it cool 10 minutes and give it to the people with milk.

title: Brined Bratwurst
instructions: In a large skillet, combine onion, balsamic vinegar, and water. Simmer over medium-low heat for 10 minutes. Place bratwurst in the simmering water; cover, and simmer 20 minutes. Be sure the water is only simmering, not boiling.
Preheat an outdoor grill for high heat, and lightly oil grate.
Remove the bratwurst, and drain liquid from pan. Add butter to the pan with the onions, and cook over low heat until onions are browned.
Grill brats over high heat for 5 to 7 minutes, turning to brown on all sides. Serve with onions.

title: Italian Beef And Rice Skillet
ingredients: 1 (2 1/4 ounce) can sliced ripe olives, drained
instructions: In 12-inch skillet, cook bacon over medium-high heat until almost crisp. Add beef, onion, bell pepper, garlic and salt; cook 5 to 7 minutes, stirring frequently, until beef is thoroughly cooked; drain.
Stir in water, tomatoes, olives and Italian seasoning; heat to boiling. Stir in rice. Reduce heat to medium; cover and simmer about 5 minutes or until rice is tender.
Sprinkle with cheese; cover and let stand until melted.

title: Canned Okra
instructions: Cover okra with water, add salt and vinegar.
Cook 5 minutes. Put in jars and seal.

title: Lemon Cut Out Cookies
instructions: In
large mixing bowl, cream together butter and sugar. Beat in
egg.
Stir dry ingredients together; add alternately with ReaLemon
to creamed mixture.
Mix well.
(Dough will be soft.) Chill overnight.

title: Creamy Rice Pudding
instructions: In a large saucepan, heat milk and salt. Add 1/2 cup rice. Cook until tender.
Add 1/4 cup evaporated milk.
Mix together 3/4 cup evaporated milk, 1/2 cup sugar, 2 eggs and vanilla.
Add slowly to hot mixture.
Cook until thick.

title: Celery Root Soup
ingredients: 2 leeks, white and light green part only, chopped
2 celery root bulbs (about a pound each), peeled and chopped
instructions: In a stock pot over medium heat, melt the butter and salute the leeks for 3-4 minutes.
Add the celery root, stock, and salt, bring to a boil, cover, and simmer for 18 minutes or until the celery root is fork tender.
Using a regular or immersion blender, carefully blend the soup until it is smooth and creamy.

To Freeze: Allow to cool, store in appropriate container or ziploc bag, label and freeze up to 4 months. When ready, defrost in fridge for 24 hours or place in pot and bring to heat over low-medium heat.

title: Vickys Spinach Pilaf and Spicy Roast Veg, Gluten, Dairy, Egg & Soy-Free
ingredients: 1 1/2 tbsp harissa paste (recipe on my profile)
2 cm square piece of ginger, finely chopped
instructions: Preheat the oven to gas 6 / 200C / 400F and line a baking tray with foil
Put the cubed squash, peppers and red onion on the tray and dot over the harissa paste.
Toss it together and roast for 40 minutes until soft and starting to char at the edges
Meanwhile put the oil in a saucepan and add in the onion, garlic, ginger and chilli.
Cook over a medium heat until the onion is translucent then add the spinach, rice and vegetable stock
Bring to the boil then turn down to a simmer, cover and cook until the stock has been absorbed into the rice, around 20 - 25 minutes
Season the pilaf with salt and pepper then plate with the roasted vegetables

title: Best Brownies
instructions: Cream butter and sugar.
Add eggs and other ingredients, beating well.
Bake in an 11 x 16-inch greased pan for 25 minutes at 350°.

title: Three Bean Salad
instructions: Drain beans and put into a large bowl.
Add onion, pepper and pimiento.
Dissolve sugar in vinegar.
Add with salt, pepper and oil.
Stir to blend.
Marinate overnight.

title: Lithuanian Meatballs
instructions: Place crackers in warmed milk.
Add to beef with salt, eggs and garlic.
Add milk to make 1/2 tablespoon rolled meatballs. Fry in medium hot Crisco until done to satisfied specifications. Do not fry in fat that is too hot.

title: Rice Casserole
instructions: Mix all ingredients in a 2-quart casserole dish. Bake 1 hour, covered, at 350°. Check to see if rice is cooked.

title: Shortcakes
instructions: Sift together first five ingredients; cut in margarine.
Stir in milk and eggs.
Mix to form a dough.
Knead dough lightly on a floured surface.
Shape into half inch thick square.
Cut into nine 3-inch biscuits.
Place on cookie sheet.
Heat oven to 400°. Bake 15 minutes.
Split warm biscuits and top with your favorite fresh or frozen fruit.
Yields 9 servings.

title: Taste of Fall, Kuri Kinton (Chestnut Paste)
ingredients: 350 grams without the shells Boiled chestnuts
instructions: Boil the chestnuts in a pressure cooker.
While the chestnuts are still hot, cut in half with a knife and scoop out the insides with a spoon.
I smashed them with a masher, but they didn't get that smashed, so I thought of putting them in a food processor, but ended up using a blender.
Mix with the sugar (1/5 of the chestnut amount) and honey in a pot over low heat.
Put saran wrap on a scale and measure out 20 g (or any size you like) of the mixture.
Wrap up in the saran wrap and you're done.

title: Stout Marinated Steak Tips
instructions: Trim silver skin and icky fatty parts from your flap meat and cut them into 3 or 4-ounce chunks, or whatever size you like.
Put them into a container with a tight fitting lid; don't forget to leave extra space for the marinade.
In a large bowl whisk together the Worcestershire sauce, soy sauce, molasses, brown sugar, pepper, pepper flakes, and garlic.
Add the stout- careful, it will foam a bit.
There is really no need to add salt; the soy and Worcestershire are plenty salty.
Pour the marinade over the tips and gently stir to coat evenly, if you don't use all the marinade it will keep for 2 weeks, refrigerated in an airtight container.
Refrigerate the meat for 8 hours or overnight.
(Will keep, refrigerated for 4 or 5 days) and then cook as desired.

title: Sig's Beetroot, Apple, Lentil and Herring Salad
ingredients: 2 salted herring filet ( or other salt cured fish)
3 small handful of tinned washed and drained puy lentils
instructions: First put your two filets of fish into the milk to get rid of some of the saltiness for about half hour .
Peel, thinly sliced and cut your beetroot into thin sticks , do not boil .
Squeeze the lemon juice oved the beetroot and stir in the sugar, add the chopped or thinly sliced onion, set aside whilst fish is being dealt ed , when nearly done peel ,decor and cube the apple.
Add the beetroot with juices , apples , onions and lentils into a serving bowl and mix together.
Drain fish and cut into mouth sized pieces add to the salad.
Sprinkle with the wild garlic and season with salt and pepper.
Add two tablespoons of creme fraiche.
Mix gently together.
I serve this on a bed of washed and dried baby spinach or wild rocket.

title: Spinach & Artichoke Ravioli With Roasted Vegetables And Tarragon Cream Sauce
ingredients: 1 cup fennel cut in half lengthwise then sliced crosswise
1 package Buitoni Refrigerated Spinach & Artichoke Ravioli 9 oz.
Buitoni Refrigerated Freshly Shredded Parmesan Cheese 5 oz.
instructions: PREHEAT oven to 400° F. Line large baking pan with foil. PLACE parsnips, carrots and fennel in prepared baking pan. Add oil and salt. Toss with hands to coat. Roast in oven for 20 minutes until parsnips and carrots are cooked but still slightly firm. PREPARE pasta according to package directions, omitting oil. Meanwhile, heat half-and-half and tarragon in a small saucepan over low heat, pressing down on tarragon to release more flavor. Drain pasta and return to saucepan. Add warm cream mixture and roasted vegetables; toss gently to combine. Serve with pepper and cheese.

title: Roasted Asparagus And Morel Mushrooms
ingredients: 1 pound asparagus spears, trimmed and halved
1 tablespoon balsamic vinegar, or to taste
instructions: Preheat the oven to 450 degrees F (230 degrees C). Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil.

Arrange asparagus and mushrooms on the prepared baking sheet. Drizzle with oil and sprinkle with salt; toss to coat. Roast, stirring occasionally, about 15 minutes. Add butter and thyme. Toss to coat, and roast an additional 5 minutes.
Arrange vegetables on a serving platter. Top with arugula, sprinkle with salt, and drizzle with balsamic vinegar.

title: Baked Ham
instructions: Place ham in large baking dish.
Sprinkle well with brown sugar.
Bake for 1 hour at 350°.
Then place pineapple slices around sides and on top.
Bake for another hour.

title: Pasta With Spinach
instructions: Cook spinach in large frypan in 1/2 cup oil with 4 or 5 large cloves garlic.
Add red pepper seed, if desired, and salt and pepper to taste.
Cook until tender; remove and set aside.
In same frypan, put in 1 cup oil, 4 or 5 cloves garlic, red pepper, capers, basil and parsley; simmer for about 5 minutes before adding tomatoes.
Simmer about 1 hour.
Pour over penne pasta which has been mixed with spinach and grated cheese.
Add some Italian black olives (if desired) before serving.

title: The Bna
instructions: Fill a large glass with ice and add brandy, maple and lemon juice. Stir until well-chilled.
Top with cider.
Make 3 more to finish the tallboy of cider before it goes flat.

title: Rootin' Tooin' Sweet & Sassy Sauce
instructions: Mix all ingredients into small saucepan.
Bring to boil & then reduce to a simmer. Cook until thickened approx 15-20 minutes.
Use immediately or store in refrigerator tightly sealed after chilling.

title: Momma Daisys Buttermilk Biscuits
ingredients: 2 1/2 cups White Lily or all-purpose flour, plus more for work surface
2 tablespoons cold lard or vegetable shortening
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425F.
Grease two baking sheets with butter.
Sift the flour, baking powder, and salt together in a large mixing bowl.
Add the lard or shortening and butter, and use your fingers to work the fat into the dry mixture until it is crumbly.
Add the buttermilk, and use your fingers or a fork to work the liquid into the flour-and-fat mixture.
After youve added all the buttermilk, you should be able to form the dough into a moist ball.
Transfer the dough to a floured work surface, and pat it into a 9- to 10-inch round.
Use a rolling pin to roll out the round 1 inch thick (adding more flour as needed to prevent sticking).
Using lightly floured 3-inch biscuit cutters, cut the dough into rounds, and transfer them to a buttered baking sheet.
Collect the dough scraps and form them into a ball; roll out a second time, and cut the dough into biscuits.
Collect the remaining scraps and pat them into a large round or hoecake.
Bake the biscuits for 10 to 12 minutes, until they are golden brown (the hoecake might take a few minutes longer).

title: Scalloped Potatoes For Two
ingredients: 2 medium potatoes, 2 1/2 cups peeled, washed, and thinly sliced
instructions: Place potatoes in a greased 1 quart baking dish, set aside.
In a small pan, melt margarine and then add flour cook until the flour taste disappears ( About 3 to 4 minutes ).
Add the milk a little at a time, bring to a boil then reduce heat and stir until slightly thickened.
Add cheese,salt, pepper, and worcestershire sauce and stir until cheese is melted.
Cool sauce slightly, pour over potatoes.
Cover potatoes and bake for 25 minutes at 375 degrees, remove cover and top with french fried onion, and paprika.
Bake for another 20 minutes or until potatoes are fork tender.
Let " rest " for 10 minutes, so sauce will thicken.

title: Cheesy Italian Tortellini
ingredients: 1 (14 1/2 ounce) can tomatoes seasoned with basil garlic & oregano, undrained
9 ounces refrigerated cheese tortellini or 9 ounces cooked cheese tortellini
instructions: Break beef and sausage into large pieces in 10-inch skillet.
Cook over medium heat about 10 minutes.
(Drain if you prefer).
Spray inside of crockpot with cooking spray.
Mix beef mixture,marinara sauce,mushrooms and tomatoes in slow cooker.
Cover and cook on low heat setting 7 to 8 hours.
Stir in tortellini: sprinkle with cheese.
Cover and cook on low heat setting about 15 minutes longer till hot or until tortellini is tender.

title: Raisin-Bran Muffins
ingredients: 1/2 C. chopped unsweetened pineapple, well drained
2 T. Canola oil, or veg. oil
instructions: In med. bowl, combine applesauce, pineapple, brown sugar, egg whites, egg, oil and vanilla. Mix well.

In another bowl, combine flour, bran flakes, raisins, nuts, cinnamon, baking powder, soda and salt. Mix well. Add applesauce mixture and stir until just blended. Spoon into lightly oiled muffin tins, 3/4 full.Bake at 350 degree oven for 15-18 min.

title: Frozen Corn
instructions: Combine corn and ingredients.
Bring to boil.
Simmer 10 minutes.
Cool, package and freeze.

title: Spiced Banana Ice Cream
ingredients: 1 whole star anise or 1/2 teaspoon star anise pieces
1/2 cup coarsely mashed ripe banana (1 medium)
an instant-read thermometer; an ice cream maker
instructions: Stir together cream, milk, brown sugar, cinnamon stick, star anise, nutmeg, cardamom, white pepper, and a pinch of salt in a 2- to 3-quart heavy saucepan. Scrape seeds from vanilla bean into cream mixture, then add pod. Puree banana in a food processor, then whisk into cream mixture. Bring just to a boil, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat and let stand, covered, 30 minutes.

Return to a boil, uncovered. Meanwhile, whisk together yolks in a large metal bowl. Add boiling-hot cream mixture in a slow stream, whisking constantly, then pour into saucepan. Cook over moderately low heat, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon, until custard is thick enough to coat back of spoon and registers 170 to 175°F on thermometer (do not let boil). Remove from heat and force custard through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean metal bowl, discarding solids. Set bowl in a larger bowl of ice water and let stand, stirring occasionally, until cold, about 30 minutes.
Freeze custard in ice cream maker, then transfer to an airtight container and harden in freezer, about 1 hour. Let soften in refrigerator 1 to 1 1/2 hours and then at room temperature 10 minutes.

title: Century Griddle Cakes
instructions: Measure and sift dry ingredients; beat eggs until light, then add milk.
Add dry ingredients to milk and eggs.
Beat thoroughly. Add melted butter.
Makes about 16 medium cakes.

title: Suzanne'S Best Pecan Pie
ingredients: 1 frozen pie shell (I use Whole Wheat)
1 tablespoon brandy or 1 tablespoon sherry wine
instructions: Preheat oven to 350.
In a saucepan, combine syrups, brown sugar, butter and vanilla. While stirring, bring to a boil so the butter and sugar dissolve. Remove from heat and add cream and brandy. Set aside.
While mixture is cooling, grind pecans in a food processor.
In a mixing bowl, beat eggs with an electric hand mixer or fork.
Add pecans and syrup mixture to the eggs and stir well with a fork.
Pour into frozen, unbaked pie shell and place on a cookie sheet.
Bake for 45 minutes.
After removing from the oven, brush the top of the pie and the crust with heavy cream.
Cool completely! Serve with fresh whipped cream if desired.
This can be made a day or 2 in advance and keeps very well covered in foil.

title: Posole Recipe
ingredients: 7 ounce. can tomato and green chile salsa
2 tbsp. beef or possibly chicken bouillon granules
instructions: In large soup kettle bring all ingredients to boil.
Lower heat and simmer, covered, about 3 hrs till posole is tender but mushy.
Stir occasionally and add in water and more seasonings if taste tells you it's necessary.
(Chilies vary in degree over hotness.)
Remove bone from ham hock when cooked and chop ham into mix.

title: " Chai " Almond Cookies
ingredients: 12 cup unsalted butter, to room temperature
34 cup toasted almond, finely chopped (NOT almond flour!)
instructions: Toast almonds and set aside to cool.
Once cooled, finely chop.
Preheat oven to 325 degrees.
In a large mixing bowl, using electric beaters, cream the butter, 1/3 cup powdered sugar, both the extracts, spices and salt.
Beat in the flour, then stir in the ground almonds.
(Tip: I found it easier to stir in the ground almonds with lightly buttered hands.
Use your hands to roll the dough into tablespoon size balls.
Place on a parchment lined baking sheet 2" apart.
Bake the cookies until light golden, about 20-25 minutes.
Cool the cookies 5 minutes on the baking sheet.
Place the remaining 1 cup of powdered sugar in a large bowl.
Working in batches, gently roll several hot cookies in the sugar.
Cool again on wire rack.
Once all cookies are cool, gently roll all of them again in the powdered sugar and serve.

title: Beef Chow Fun Recipe
ingredients: 1 lb Beef flank steak, sliced across the grain
1 Tbsp. Fermented black beans (dow, see), rinsed
1/2 x Green sweet bell pepper, cored & cut julienne
1 c. Fresh bean sprouts Chinese parsley, (garnish)
instructions: Slice the chow fun into noodles about 1/2 inch wide.
Slice the meat and mix the marinade.
Marinate the meat for 15 min.
Heat the wok and add in
2 Tablespoons of the peanut oil.
Toss the noodles about in the oil till they are very warm and begin to color just a bit on the edges.
Remove to the serving platter and set aside.
Heat the wok again and add in 3 Tablespoons of the peanut oil along with the garlic and ginger.
Chow for a moment and add in the rinsed black beans and the sherry.
In this chow the onion and green pepper, just till very warm.
Remove to the serving platter.
Heat the wok
a third time and add in 2 Tablespoons of the peanut oil and the meat.
Chow on one side
only till it begins to brown.
Return the vegetables, the noodles, and the sauce to the wok and toss with the meat till warm.
Add in the bean sprouts, toss just a minute or possibly so and serve.
Garnish with Chinese parsley.

title: Ham, Potatoes, And Green Beans
ingredients: 1 -2 lb fresh green beans or 1 -2 lb frozen green beans
instructions: Cube the potatoes, peeling first if desired.
String the beans and break into 2 inch pieces.
Coarsely chop the onion.
Place ham, potatoes, beans, black pepper, and onions into a wide, shallow pot or dutch oven.
Add enough water to come within a couple inches of the top of the food.
Bring the pot to a boil then simmer gently for 15 minutes or until potatoes are tender.
This can be drippy so I suggest serving it in bowls or soup plates.
Cornbread makes a good accompaniment if you want to sop up the juices.

title: Make-Ahead Motuleno
instructions: Cook bacon in medium skillet until crisp.
Remove bacon from skillet with slotted spoon, reserving 2 Tbsp.
drippings in skillet.
Drain bacon on paper towels.
Add plantains to reserved drippings; cook on medium heat 3 min.
or until golden brown, stirring frequently.
Stir in peas; cook 2 min.
Remove from heat; stir in bacon.
Spread beans onto tortillas; place in single layer on foil-covered baking sheet.
Top with Singles and plantain mixture.
Use back of spoon to make well in center of plantain mixture on each tortilla; cover with foil.
Refrigerate up to 24 hours.
Heat oven to 350 degrees F. Crack egg into each well.
Bake, covered, 20 to 25 min.
or until egg whites are completely set and yolks begin to thicken but do not become firm.

title: Pumpkin Pie Crumble
instructions: Add pumpkin, milk, eggs, sugar and spice together.
Pour into greased 8 x 13-inch pan.
Sprinkle box of dry cake mix over the pumpkin mixture covering completely.
Sprinkle nuts over cake mixture. Drizzle top of nuts with melted butter and remaining top. Bake at 350° for 1 hour; cool completely before cutting into squares.
Can top with whipped cream.
Really delicious.

title: Vegetable Stir Fry
ingredients: canola cooking spray or canola oil
instructions: Rinse and cut up all the vegetables.
Slice tofu into bite-sized pieces and drain in a sieve.
Lightly spray or oil the fry pan with Canola oil/spray and place the pan over medium high heat.
Add onion and garlic and saute for 3 minutes; do not let them burn.
Add remaining vegetables but save the asparagus for last.
Add chicken bouillion and cooking wine.
Cover fry pan.
Stir frequently and cook until desired tenderness.
Create a circle in the middle of the pan by pushing the vegetables aside.
Drop in the tofu and turn as needed.
Add asparagus and sprinkle herbs over the vegetables.
Enjoy!

title: Jan's Pie Crust
instructions: Whip the first 3 ingredients with a fork until it's all creamy.
Add the last 2 ingredients and put into a dough ball with your hands.
Roll it out between 2 sheets of wax paper.
Put into pie pan and roll under the edges on top evenly, making ridges using your 2 fingers, around the top.
Prick the whole the dough all around with a fork so the air can escape while baking unless it is for a pumpkin pie, because no pre-baking is necessary.
Bake at 450 degrees.
After 5 minutes, if the crust is bubbling up, prick it more with the fork.
Bake it for about 15 minutes till golden brown.

title: Sunday's Gourmet Chicken, Mozzarella, Basil and Tomato Sandwich
ingredients: 3 ounces fresh mozzarella cheese (just use enough healthy yet even slices of it to cover the chicken breast)
1 piece ciabatta (split in half to make sandwich bread)
instructions: Take one piece of Ciabatta bread, and place the grilled chicken on it.
Arrange the FRESH Mozzarella slices on top of the chicken, enough to cover it.

Place the sandwich half and other half of Ciabatta (enough to toast for the plain slice, and be sure to choose now if you are using the olive oil, or the dijon and balsamic) in a broiler or toaster oven enough to melt the mozzarella, and make it a little bubbly!
If using the olive oil, brush the top Ciabatta piece with enough to cover.
While waiting for the mozzarella to get bubbly, mix the balsamic vinegar (enough or to your own taste to spread on the other piece of Ciabatta bread) with some of the Dijon Mustard.
Be careful, because the Dijon Vinagrette will be to your own tastes.
Usually 3 tablespoons of the balsamic to 1 tablespoon Dijon works for me.
You can use this or just use extra virgin olive oil on the top piece.
Take your chicken and Ciabatta out of the broiler or toaster oven.
Place the whole basil on the mozzarella cheese.
Place your sliced tomato on top of the whole basil.
Place the chopped basil on top of the tomato.
Now, either use the Dijon Vinaigrette on the top slice, or if you used the oil, just top off your sandwich!
Enjoy!

title: Jo'S Broccoli Casserole
ingredients: 2 pkg. chopped broccoli, thawed and drained well
2 c. cooked and cubed chicken (whole fryer)
instructions: Thaw and drain broccoli.
Cook and cube chicken.
Mix together mayonnaise, cream of chicken soup and lemon juice.
Spread broccoli on bottom of a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Then add cubed chicken, then soup mixture.
Sprinkle on top Pepperidge Farm herb dressing mix.
Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes, not covered.
Bake until almost done, then add cheese so it won't be hard.

title: Stuffed Chicken Breasts With Sherry Sauce
ingredients: 2 whole chicken breasts, split, boned and skinned
instructions: Pound each half chicken breast with flat side of meat mallet to 1/4 inch thickness. Form butter into 4 balls.

Roll balls in mixture of chives, parsley, garlic, salt and pepper. Place 1 seasoned ball on each slice of Swiss cheese. Place cheese on chicken breast; roll so butter is completely enclosed. Secure with toothpicks. Heat oil in frying pan; brown the chicken rolls about 8 minutes. Place into covered, shallow baking dish. Preheat oven to 350°.

title: Blue Cheese Dip
instructions: Mix together and refrigerate.
Serve with cup veggies or chips or crackers.
Keeps well.

title: Sweet Potato Casserole
instructions: Bake potatoes until tender and caramelized (about 45 min @ 400 degrees).
Take skin off when cooled and able to handle.
Put in a large bowl and mash with butter.
Pour into a buttered casserole and top with the pecan topping.
Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes.

title: Poached Fish Low Cholesterol Recipe
instructions: In a large shallow pan, saute/fry the onion and celery in oil till tender.
Place skinned fillets on top of vegetables, or possibly roll each fillet, secure with a toothpick and place on vegetables.
Add in water and seasonings.
Cover and simmer about 8 min or possibly till fish flakes when tested with a fork.
Remove and serve with Lemon Parsley Sauce or possibly Horseradish Sauce.

title: Chocolate Butterscotch Haystacks
instructions: In a microwave or large metal bowl over simmering water, melt chocolate chips and butterscotch chips; stir until smooth. Gently stir in noodles.
Drop by rounded tablespoonfuls onto waxed paper-lined
. Refrigerate 10-15 minutes or until set.

title: German Beets
instructions: Simmer onions in melted butter until tender.
Stir in sugar, cornstarch and salt.
Add water and vinegar.
Add beets and simmer for 30 minutes to blend flavors.

title: Martha’S Fish Tacos
ingredients: 1 medium ripe avocado, peeled and chopped
instructions: Spread corn with butter and sprinkle with salt and pepper. Grill, covered, over medium heat for 10-12 minutes or until tender, turning occasionally.
Meanwhile, sprinkle fish with 1 teaspoon chili powder. Lightly oil the grill rack. Grill fish, covered, over medium heat for 7-9 minutes or until fish flakes easily with a fork.

Cool corn slightly; remove kernels from cobs. Place in a large bowl. Add the lettuce, tomatoes, red pepper, avocado, taco sauce, 1 tablespoon lime juice, cilantro, lime zest, and remaining chili powder.
Drizzle remaining lime juice over fish; cut into 1/2-in. cubes.
Add to corn mixture. Spoon 1/2 cup mixture over each tortilla. Serve immediately.

title: Buttermilk Ranch Oven "Fried" Chicken
ingredients: 1 hidden valley buttermilk ranch dressing (dry ingredients)
instructions: Heat oven to 425°F Melt butter in rectangular baking dish, 13x9x2 inches, in oven.

In a gallon plastic bag, mix together Bisquick, and Hidden Valley Buttermilk Ranch dressing. Coat chicken one piece at a time, but placing in bag and shaking. Place skin sides down in dish (dish and butter should be hot).
Bake 35 minutes; turn chicken. Bake about 15 minutes longer or until juice is no longer pink when centers of thickest pieces are cut.

title: Barbecue Sauce
instructions: Melt butter.
Add onion and green pepper; cook slowly for 2 minutes.
Add remaining ingredients; simmer for 10 minutes. Yields 8 servings.

title: Irish Boxty
instructions: In a clean cloth, place the grated raw potatoes and twist to remove excess moisture.
Whisk together flour, salt, and baking powder. Combine flour mixture into raw potatoes, mashed potatoes, and eggs. Add enough mix to make a batter.

Heat a heavy skillet over medium heat and add butter or oil. Drop potato batter by the tablespoon into the hot pan. Brown on both sides (about 4 minutes per side). Butter each boxty and serve hot with or without sugar.

title: Tourtiere(Pork Pie)
instructions: Combine meats, onion, salt and water.
Cook slowly for 45 minutes.
Add spices; cook 15 minutes longer.
Let stand to let flavors blend.
Cook potatoes.
Mash while hot; mix immediately with meat mixture.
Place in a pie shell.
Cover with top shell; lattice style if desired.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes or until nicely browned.
When cooled, may be stored in freezer, wrapped in tinfoil or adequate freezer bag.
A delightful dish.
Always draws compliments from guests and family.

title: Grits Casserole
instructions: Cook grits, salt, water and milk until thick.
Add margarine and cheese.
Beat eggs and add to grits mixture.
Stir until cheese melts and no white grits show.
Bake at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes.

title: Stuffed Mushrooms
instructions: Fry mushrooms in butter and oil; set aside.
With oil and butter left over, add onion and stems of mushrooms and fry.
Mix the bread crumbs, chives and bacon bits to onion and mushroom stems.
Add sour cream; mix altogether.
Fill mushrooms and broil when ready to eat.

title: Artichoke Rice Salad(Serves 6 To 8)
ingredients: 1 pkg. chicken Rice-A-Roni, cooked as directed
instructions: Put Rice-A-Roni into bowl to cool.
Add onions and olives to rice.
Drain artichoke hearts into bowl.
Combine with marinade the curry powder and mayonnaise.
Add to rice mixture.
Mix well. Cover and refrigerate until chilled.
This can be made ahead of time and kept in the refrigerator until ready to serve.

title: Apple Plus
instructions: Cream butter and margarine.
Add sugar and blend well.
Beat in eggs.
Mix in vanilla and flour (that has been sifted with soda and salt).
Add apples, pecans, coconut and dates.
Bake in 9 x 5 x 3-inch loaf pan for 1 hour at 300°, then 25 minutes at 325°. Test to see if it is done.

title: Blackberry Zima Lemonade Recipe
instructions: Ice (shake) // rocks

title: Fruit Salad
instructions: Fold pudding into Cool Whip.
Drain all canned fruit.
Add remaining ingredients.

title: Soup For A Winter Evening
instructions: Mix all ingredients and pour in crock-pot.
Cook on low for 3 to 4 hours.

title: Linguine
instructions: In skillet, brown garlic in 1/4 cup of oil.
Add clams and one clam can of water.
Add parsley, salt and pepper and bring to a boil for 3 to 4 minutes.
Turn off heat.
Cook linguine according to package directions.
Drain.
Serve clam sauce over linguine.

title: Pecan Pie (Ingredients For 2 Pies) Recipe
ingredients: 5 lg. or possibly 6 med. Large eggs
instructions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
In saucepan combine sugar and syrup, stir over medium heat till it begins to boil.
Remove from heat and stir in butter till dissolved.
In large mixing bowl beat Large eggs.
Pour sugar syrup mix into mixing bowl with Large eggs and beat.
Add in pecans and vanilla and stir.
Pour in pie shells.
Bake at 350 degrees appoximately 40 to 45 min.

title: Beef Shish Kabobs
ingredients: 1 large green pepper, cut into 1-inch squares
instructions: Combine first 6 ingredients in a bowl.
Add beef cubes. Cover and refrigerate at least four hours or overnight, stirring occasionally.

title: Crock Pot Italian Zucchini
ingredients: 1 cup water or 1 cup vegetable broth
instructions: Put all ingredients in the crock pot and cook on low for 6-8 hours.

title: Thanksgiving Bites over Fresh Cranberry Sauce
ingredients: 2 whole Apples, Cored And Finely Diced
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F.
In a large bowl, thoroughly combine all of the meatball ingredients, form into your preferred size meatballs, and line them up on a rimmed baking sheet.
Place in oven and cook uncovered for 40 minutes, until the chicken is thoroughly cooked and the meatballs are golden brown.
While the meatballs are baking, prepare the cranberry sauce.
Place the cranberries, water, and sugar in a medium saucepan over medium-low heat.
Using a whisk, combine ingredients.
Bring to a boil and whisk again.
Continue to cook for an additional 10 minutes until the cranberries have burst and the sauce is formed.
Remove from heat.
To plate, place desired amount of cranberry sauce on the plate and top with meatballs.

title: Stuffed Mushrooms
instructions: Saute peppers and onions in olive oil.
Cook sausage.
Add sausage to green peppers and onions.
Add Italian bread crumbs. Remove stems from mushrooms.
Stuff with filling.
Top with cheese.
Bake at 350° until cheese is melted.

title: Sweet Potato Casserole
ingredients: 2 1/2 lb. sweet potatoes (may use precooked, canned to equal 4 small cans)
instructions: Melt margarine and mix with brown sugar, flour and chopped pecans.
Sprinkle over sweet potato mixture and bake for 25 to 35 minutes in 350° oven.

title: Split Pea Soup
instructions: Combine peas and water.
Bring to a boil and boil 2 minutes. Remove from heat.
Cover and let set for 1 hour.
Add remaining ingredients.
Bring to boiling point.
Reduce heat and simmer 2 1/2 to 3 hours or until peas look creamed and ham hock is tender. Remove ham hock.
Trim meat from bone and dice.
Return meat to soup.
Heat thoroughly and serve.
Additional water or milk may be added to soup for a thinner consistency.

title: Cheesy Brussels Sprouts
ingredients: 1-1/2 cups Cracker Barrel Shredded Old Cheddar Cheese
instructions: Cook Brussels sprouts in saucepan of boiling water 8 to 10 min.
or until tender.
Meanwhile, melt butter in large saucepan on low heat.
Whisk in flour; cook and stir 1 min.
Gradually whisk in milk until blended; cook on medium heat 3 to 5 min.
or until thickened, whisking constantly.
Add cheese; cook and stir 1 min.
or until melted.
Pour cheese sauce into serving bowl.
Drain Brussels sprouts; spoon over cheese sauce.
Sprinkle with nuts.

title: 100% Parmesan Chicken
ingredients: 2 to 3 Tbsp. melted margarine or butter
instructions: Heat oven to 400°.
Spray baking pan with nonstick spray.
Dip chicken in butter, then in the remaining ingredients mixed together.
Bake for 20 to 25 minutes.

title: Chicken Adobo
instructions: Combine all ingredients and heat to a boil.
Stir chicken pieces.
Reduce heat to a simmer and cook for one hour, stirring chicken occasionally.
Remove chicken and thicken sauce with a mixture of cornstarch and water.
Serve with rice and a salad.

title: Slice 'N' Bake Coffee Cookies
ingredients: 3/4 cup toasted and skinned whole hazelnuts, chopped
instructions: Beat butter at medium speed of an electric mixer until creamy; gradually add 1 cup sugar, beating well. Add egg and vanilla, beating until blended.
Combine flour, baking powder, and salt; add to butter mixture, beating well. Stir in hazelnuts. Divide dough in half; shape into 11" logs.
Combine 1/4 cup sugar and coffee granules; stir well. Roll each log in sugar mixture, coating heavily. Wrap each log in wax paper. Chill logs until firm.

Cut dough into 1/4" thick slices, and place on ungreased cookie sheets. Bake at 350° for 8 to 10 minutes or until lightly browned. Remove to wire racks, and cool completely. Store in an airtight container up to 1 week.

title: Cheesy Rice with Side Salad
ingredients: 2 slices KRAFT 2% Milk Sharp Cheddar Singles
1/2 cup sliced frozen carrots Target 2 lb For $3.00 thru 02/06
1 tsp. trans fat free canola-based soft margarine
1 medium tomato, cut into wedges Safeway 1 lb For $1.29 thru 02/09
instructions: Boil 1/3 cup water; stir in rice and 2% Milk Singles.
Cook and stir until melted.
Remove from heat; cover.
Let stand 5 min., then stir again.
Heat carrots as directed on package.
Top with margarine.
Toss lettuce and tomatoes.
Drizzle with oil and vinegar; season as desired with favorite herbs.
Serve meal with a glass of milk.

title: Kolache
ingredients: 1 small yeast or 1 pkg. dry yeast
filling (such as poppy seed, almond or walnut)
instructions: Combine yeast and water.
Set aside.
Cream butter and sugar together, then add eggs, 1 at a time.
Add sour cream, then yeast mixture.
Add flour.
Mix well.

title: Lemon Lust
instructions: Combine first 3 ingredients into a dough.
Press into a 13 x 9-inch pan.
Bake for 15 minutes at 375°.
Mix sugar, cream cheese and 1 cup of the Cool Whip.
Spread on crust.
Mix milk and pudding mix; spread over creamed cheese mixture.
Top with balance of Cool Whip.
Chill well.

title: Grandma'S Jello Salad(Childrens Recipe)
instructions: Mix first three ingredients together with Jello. Mix well. Stir in marshmallows.
Serve in ice cream cones or cups.
Great for grandchildren.

title: Diana’S Blueberry Dessert
instructions: Dissolve jello in boiling water.
Add in the pineapple, blueberry pie filling, and pecans, and mix thoroughly and pour into a glass casserole dish. Let the fruit salad congeal.
Blend the cream cheese, sour cream, sugar, and vanilla to form topping. After salad is congealed, spread evenly on top. Refrigerate.
Optional: Before serving, use some fresh blueberries to garnish the top!

title: Barefoot Contessa'S Plum Raspberry Crumble
ingredients: 2 lbs red plums, cut in 1/2, pitted, and cut in 1-inch wedges
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
In a large bowl, combine the sliced plums, 1/3 cup of the granulated sugar, 1/4 cup of the flour, and the orange juice and toss well.
Add the raspberries and toss lightly.
Pour into a 9 by 12 by 2-inch baking dish.

For the topping, place the remaining 1 cup of flour, the remaining 1/3 cup of granulated sugar, the brown sugar, and the salt in the bowl of a food processor fitted with a steel blade and pulse a few times to combine.
Add the butter and pulse until the butter is the size of peas.
Pour the mixture into a bowl, add the oats, and work it with your hands until it's in large crumbles.
Add 1/2 cup of the almonds and mix well.
Spread the topping evenly over the plums, making sure the fruit is covered.
Sprinkle with some extra almonds.
Bake for 40 minutes, or until the fruit is tender and bubbly and the topping is golden brown.
Serve warm or at room temperature.

title: Beef Stew
instructions: Place every ingredient into a large stock pot.
Turn heat on stove top to low/medium low heat.
Cover and let simmer for at least 5 hours.
Once simmered for at least 5 hours, remove bones from pot.
Make sure to leave the marrow from the bones in the pot.
Use a pair of tongs or fork to shred the tenderized meat.
Leave uncovered and turn heat up to medium-high.
Let it cook off the excess liquid and reduce down to desired liquid level.
This usually takes 30-45 minutes.
Once desired liquid is cooked off, serve over mashed potatoes or in a bread bowl.

title: Perfect Sea Scallops
instructions: Rinse scallops in cold water and pat dry with paper towels.
Combine butter and basil in a small bowl.
Using your hands, roll the mixture into a ball.
Season scallops with salt and pepper on both sides.
In a large skillet, heat oil over high heat.
Add scallops (its better when they are not touching each other, cook in batches if necessary) and sear for 1 1/2 minutes on each side.
Move scallops to one side of the skillet and add the butter.
Cook scallops for another 30 seconds, spooning the melted butter over them.
Serve immediately.

title: Tequila Orange Glazed Grilled Pineapple
ingredients: 1 large fresh pineapple, peeled, cored and cut into 1 inch thick slices
1 teaspoon of fresh mint, finely sliced
instructions: Mix tequila, marmalade, sugar, butter, vanilla, cinnamon and mint together heat till sugar is dissolved.
Preheat Grill.
Place pineapple on grill.
Baste with tequila mixture while grilling.
Turn occasionally.
Grill about 10 to 15 minutes.
Serve hot with a scoop of your favorite ice cream.

title: Vegetable Enchiladas
ingredients: 1 cup mixed vegetables, sliced (any combination of vegetables such as: small Japanese sliced eggplant, cabbage, cucumber (asian enlich or burp), asparagus, carrots, beans, etc.)
5 large tomatoes, peeled and diced or one 24-ounce can whole tomatoes, drained and chopped
1 cup lima beans, soaked overnight and drained
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Lightly spray vegetables with a cooking spray.
Place vegetables on a nonstick baking dish and broil until brown, about 3 minutes.
Allow vegetables to cool.
Combine tomatoes, basil, garlic, tomato paste, brown sugar, salt and pepper in a saucepan and heat.
Add vegetables and simmer about 10 minutes.
Meanwhile in a small saucepan, combine White Bean Gravy with white wine and heat thoroughly.
Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Fill tortillas with the vegetable mixture, then fold into enchiladas.
Place in a baking dish and bake for about 20 minutes or until enchiladas are brown.
Before serving, cover with heated gravy.
Serve with beans and rice.

1 cup lima beans, soaked overnight and drained 1 1/2 cups water 1/4 stick (1-ounce) butter 1/2 medium onion, diced 1/2 cup heavy cream 1 teaspoon cornstarch 2 tablespoons water Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste

title: Crunchy Peanut-Butter Cookies With Shredded Wheat
instructions: Mix together the butter, peanut butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar until creamy.
Add eggs and vanilla and beat until light.
Sift the flour with the soda and salt; stir into creamed mixture until well blended.
Lightly mix in the shredded wheat and peanuts.

Drop rounded teaspoonfuls of batter, about 2 inches apart, on ungreased cookie sheets. (This makes rather ragged cookies, so I like to roll them into balls first, but little pieces of shredded wheat DO stab a bit!) Using the tines of a fork, press dough flat in each direction to make a crisscross pattern on top of each cookie.
Bake in a 350 degree oven for about 10 to 12 minutes or until lightly browned.
Makes about 8 dozen cookies.

title: Zucchini Raisin Spice Cake
instructions: In a large bowl, beat together the eggs, brown sugar, oil and vanilla.
Beat in the dry ingredients and mix well.
Stir in the zucchini and raisins.
Pour into a greased 8-inch square pan.
Bake in preheated 350° oven about 40 to 45 minutes or until done.
When cool, dust with confectioners sugar.
Serves 8.

title: Healthy Apple Butter
ingredients: 8 lbs apples, peeled, cored and diced
instructions: Place apples in a blender or food processor with apple juice and blend until smooth. Transfer to the slow cooker, set on low and cook for 6 to 7 hours. Stir occasionally. Let mixture cool; reblend and stir in spices. If desired, add more spices to taste. Store in the refrigerator after cooling to room temperature.

title: Pumpkin Chipotle “Stoup” With Pinto Beans
ingredients: 2 whole Chipotle Peppers In Adobo, Chopped Small
2 cups Pinto Beans, Cooked Or Canned
instructions: 1. Saute onion and green pepper in olive oil until soft, about 5-7 minutes, seasoning with salt and pepper. Then add chopped garlic and cook for another minute.
2. Add chopped tomatoes, chipotle peppers in adobo, canned pumpkin, pinto beans and water.
3. Stir and cover, simmering for 15-20 minutes so the flavors combine.
4. Serve, with plain Greek yogurt as a topping if you so desire!

title: Zucchini Pickles
instructions: Put zucchini, onions and peppers in a large container and sprinkle with 1/2 cup coarse salt.
Cover with 4 trays of ice cubes and let stand 3 to 4 hours.

title: Pickles
instructions: In a large 1/2 gallon container, alternate sliced cucumbers and onions.
Dissolve sugar and salt in vinegar; pour over pickles.

title: Clamato® Cooked Salmon
